name: wp13-live-materialized-prompt-proof
goal: Use the local orchestrator on a small real chained test change that proves reviewed dependency materialization is workable in a downstream implementer workspace.

sharedContext.summary: Run one bounded live chained workflow beyond the dry-run materialization sample. Focus on the operator-visible prompt and manifest surfaces for dependency materialization, keep the change narrow, and prove that a downstream implementer can continue from reviewed upstream test state inside its own isolated workspace.
constraints: Keep changes tightly scoped to the Python orchestrator test suite unless a minimal coordinator code touch is required.
Do not edit TODO.md, ai/state files, ai/log files, or ai/indexes.

tasks.title: Inspect the live materialization proof gap
tasks.prompt: Read the orchestrator script, guide/spec, and Python tests. Identify the smallest missing regression coverage that would make the new dependency-materialization contract operator-visible in tests, especially around worker prompts, validate output, or manifest/task-record serialization. Recommend a narrow two-step implementation path that can be split across two sequential implementer tasks without broadening scope.

tasks.title: Add first dependency-materialization regression
tasks.prompt: Implement the first half of the narrowest coherent regression coverage for dependency materialization. Prefer focused additions in `scripts/tests/test_claude_orchestrator.py` that prove the operator-visible contract around `dependencyMaterialization`, `dependency_materialization_mode`, or prompt rendering. Keep the change small, avoid unrelated cleanup, and include at most one high-signal structured validation intent if you make a real change.

tasks.title: Extend the regression from reviewed upstream state
tasks.prompt: Continue from the reviewed upstream workspace state and complete the same regression slice. Tighten or extend the tests so they prove the live dependency-materialization contract more clearly end to end, such as applied-layer visibility or the `apply-reviewed` prompt path. Work from the task workspace as the source of truth, keep the scope narrow, and change coordinator code only if the tighter regression exposes a real mismatch.

tasks.title: Review the chained materialization proof
tasks.prompt: Review the dependency outputs and changed files. Focus on whether the new regression coverage actually proves the live dependency-materialization contract operators care about, whether the chained implementer split stayed coherent, and whether the suggested validation is sufficient. Findings first: call out bugs, regressions, or missing coverage before any summary.
